reasoning: Since the fetch_ts_yfinance tool returned no data and week-to-week stock movements are essentially random walks with a slight upward drift due to the equity market premium, the probability of the stock closing higher one week later is driven primarily by the base rate. The historical probability of an arbitrary US stock showing a positive return over a one-week period is approximately 52-54%. Given the lack of specific momentum or volatility data to adjust this baseline, I will maintain the base rate estimate of 53%.
